description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined, angular fuselage with a prominent main rotor mounted on top and a tail boom extending to a vertical stabilizer. The body is predominantly dark, with a matte finish, and the main rotor consists of two long, straight blades. The cockpit is integrated into the front section, but the windows are not clearly visible due to the dark coloration. The tail section includes a vertical fin and a small, horizontal stabilizer, with a tail rotor mounted at the end. The landing gear appears to be a pair of skids, providing stable ground support for urban landings.
description: A dark, angular helicopter with a two-blade main rotor, tail rotor, and skid-type landing gear. The body is streamlined and matte, suited for urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, central rotor hub, and symmetrical skids.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, main rotor, and tail boom with vertical stabilizer.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail rotor, vertical fin, and the width of the skids.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, mirroring view 1, with clear visibility of the cockpit area and rotor alignment.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored dark gray or black, with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present at the end of the tail boom, appears to have two blades oriented vertically, colored dark, with no visible markings or protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Pair of straight, parallel landing skids, likely made of metal or composite, colored black, with simple support struts and no visible crossbars or footpads.
